task: I’m putting together a short project handoff bio and want it to sound plain and professional. What details usually belong in a brief intro so the next person knows who’s responsible and what they’re walking into?

turns.agent_response:
Keep it to these basics:

- **Project name / location**
- **Current status**
- **Who’s responsible now**
- **Key open items**
- **Next step**
- **Any hold point or deadline**
- **Who to contact for questions**

### Simple format
> Project: [name/location]  
> Status: [what’s done / what’s in progress]  
> Responsible: [name/company]  
> Open items: [list]  
> Next step: [what happens next]  
> Hold point/deadline: [if any]  
> Contact: [name/phone]

### Rule
If it affects **scope, money, schedule, or signoff**, it belongs in the intro.

If you want, I can turn that into a **one-paragraph handoff bio** template.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that covers it. Plain and professional is the right lane.

For a short handoff intro, keep it to:
- project name and location
- where the job stands right now
- who’s on point
- what’s still open
- next step
- any deadline, inspection, or hold-up
- who to call if something goes sideways

If it touches scope, money, schedule, or signoff, put it in. Everything else is noise.

That simple format you laid out works fine.
